items.title: SEK: Softer inflation may delay Riksbank hikes – TD Securities
items.summary: TD Securities analysts note Swedish CPIF and CPIF ex-Energy inflation for March surprised sharply to the downside, driven mainly by weaker Food and Recreation, Sport & Culture prices, partly offset by petrol.

items.title: Fed's Williams: Iran war impact to drive up headline inflation
items.summary: Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams told Bloomberg on Tuesday that the impact of the Iran war will drive up headline inflation.

items.title: RBNZ: Constrained as energy shock bites – BBH
items.summary: Brown Brothers Harriman’s (BBH) Elias Haddad expects the Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) to keep the OCR at 2.25%, with Governor Breman set to update growth and inflation projections.

items.title: S&P 500: Resilience with limited stagflation fallout – Deutsche Bank
items.summary: Deutsche Bank’s Henry Allen argues that the S&P 500’s modest pullback versus past Oil shocks reflects markets pricing a short conflict, resilient macro data and still‑dovish central banks.

items.title: USD: Dovish Fed risks and ceasefire repricing – Societe Generale
items.summary: Societe Generale’s Kit Juckes discusses Robin Brooks’ view that the Dollar looks significantly overvalued versus G10 rate differentials and could fall sharply on a ceasefire, with Oil tumbling and safe-haven flows reversing.

items.title: AUD/NZD: Diverging flows and policy risks – BNY
items.summary: BNY’s Head of Markets Macro Strategy Bob Savage highlights a growing divergence between the New Zealand Dollar (NZD) and Australian Dollar (AUD) as markets favor currencies backed by real assets.

items.title: US: Fed data watch shapes rate outlook – BBH
items.summary: Brown Brothers Harriman’s (BBH) Elias Haddad highlights a busy US data calendar for the Dollar, with February PCE, March CPI and the University of Michigan survey guiding inflation expectations.

items.title: WTI Crude Oil steadies above $103.00 with Iran’s deadline looming
items.summary: The US benchmark West Texas Intermediate Oil (WTI) hesitates near four-week highs on Tuesday. Price action remains steady above the 103.00 per barrel, but attempts to extend gains beyond 105.00 have been capped with US President Donald Trump’s deadline on Tehran approaching.

items.title: Brent: Conflict scenarios keep prices elevated – Societe Generale
items.summary: Societe Generale’s Commodity Compass Analytics (CCA) team, led by Michael Haigh, Ben Hoff and Jeremy Sellem, highlights that Dated Brent at $141/bbl reflects severe physical tightness as Strait of Hormuz flows remain impaired.

items.title: Dow Jones futures slip as traders adopt caution ahead of Trump’s Iran deadline
items.summary: Dow Jones futures fall 0.2% to near 46,800 during European hours on Tuesday, ahead of the regular United States (US) open. Meanwhile, S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 futures also decline 0.34% and 0.45% to near 6,620 and 24,250, respectively, at the time of writing.

items.title: ECB’s Wunsch: I'm going to the meeting in April being open
items.summary: European Central Bank (ECB) policymaker and the head of Belgium's central bank, Pierre Wunsch, said in the Wall Street Journal’s (WSJ) What's News podcast conducted on Monday and released on Tuesday that the central bank could deliver an interest rate hike in the April meeting to limit the effect of

items.title: DXY: Bracing for deadline risk – ING
items.summary: ING’s Chris Turner notes the Dollar (USD) remains supported as investors await a White House deadline linked to the US-Iran conflict and elevated energy prices. Strong US jobs data and resilient activity could see markets price Federal Reserve (Fed) hikes if Oil rises further.
